title: SVO Word Order
description: Learn the basic English sentence structure: Subject-Verb-Object. In ISL, sentences often follow SOV order. English requires SVO.
learning_objectives: Understand that English uses Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) word order
Recognize the difference between ISL (SOV) and English (SVO) order
Build simple SVO sentences using gestures

intro_content.title: English Word Order: Subject → Verb → Object
intro_content.explanation: In English, sentences follow a strict order: first WHO (Subject), then WHAT THEY DO (Verb), then TO WHAT (Object). In ISL, the order is often SOV (Subject-Object-Verb). When learning English, it's important to put the verb BEFORE the object.

examples.rule: SVO: Subject first, then Verb, then Object

examples.error: This is SOV order (ISL pattern) — in English, the verb comes before the object

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: I grab apple.
challenges.context_description: A person reaching for an apple
challenges.target_concept_explanation: Place the Subject first, then the Verb, then the Object.
hints: Start with the Subject: Who is doing the action?
Next comes the Verb: What action are they doing?
Finally, the Object: What are they acting on?

SUBJECT_I|APPLE|GRAB.feedback: You used ISL word order (SOV)! In English, the verb comes BEFORE the object: 'I grab apple', not 'I apple grab'.
SUBJECT_I|APPLE|GRAB.rule_reference: English uses SVO order: Subject → Verb → Object
SUBJECT_I|APPLE|GRAB.gesture_hint: After signing I, sign GRAB next, then APPLE

GRAB|SUBJECT_I|APPLE.feedback: The verb should not come first. Start with the Subject (I), then Verb (grab), then Object (apple).
GRAB|SUBJECT_I|APPLE.rule_reference: English SVO: Subject always comes first.

APPLE|SUBJECT_I|GRAB.feedback: This is topic-fronting (ISL pattern). In English, the subject comes first, not the object.
APPLE|SUBJECT_I|GRAB.rule_reference: Avoid placing the object at the beginning of the sentence.

challenges.context_description: A person holding up their hand to stop
challenges.target_concept: SV Order (intransitive verb — no object needed)
challenges.target_concept_explanation: Some verbs like 'stop' don't need an object. The sentence is just Subject + Verb.
hints: This sentence only needs two words.
'Stop' is intransitive — it doesn't need an object.

STOP|SUBJECT_I.feedback: The verb comes after the subject in English. Say 'I stop', not 'stop I'.
STOP|SUBJECT_I.rule_reference: English SVO: Subject first, then Verb.

key_takeaways: English sentences follow SVO order: Subject → Verb → Object
ISL often uses SOV order — be aware of this transfer pattern
Some verbs (intransitive) don't need an object
common_mistakes: Putting the object before the verb (ISL SOV transfer)
Putting the object at the start (ISL topic-fronting)
Starting with the verb instead of the subject
